summary: Unmerged WIP matching batch reporting 34 matched functions and 7 failed attempts, later closed as replaced by PRs #2322, #2323, #2324, and #2325. The provided diff slice replaces multiple `/// #...` stubs with C implementations across Kirby special-move code, game-mode/results/CSS logic, Icicle Mountain and Kongo Jungle stage helpers, name-entry logic, and toy/trophy input code. Reusable value is mostly in matching tactics: tightening header prototypes, adding precise includes, preserving MWCC-sensitive control flow and register allocation patterns, reloading `GET_FIGHTER` after state changes, and using exact f32/f64 distance behavior.

key_files.why: Largest fighter change; matched Kirby Neutral-B/Final Cutter helpers including `ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter`, `ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8`, and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8` in the diff excerpt.
Matched Kirby copied Jigglypuff Rollout collision/turnaround helpers: `ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll`, `ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll`, and `fn_80105978`.
Matched `fn_80170110`, a post-match bonus decision-table lookup with typed routing to player-specific or game-wide bonus handlers.
Matched `gm_801BEDA8`, cycling VS/CSS match type forward or backward and copying CSS data to `VsModeData`.
Matched `fn_8017F09C`, a regular-clear time bonus calculation gated by result/match flags and saturated at zero.
Renamed/exported `FUN_801f91ec` as `grIceMt_801F91EC`, corrected signature, and changed animation setup arguments.
Matched `grKongo_801D8078`, scanning items for a nearby barrel cannon kind using squared distance from the stage reference position.
Matched and typed `IsNameUnique`, iterating persistent name slots and comparing name strings.
Matched and typed `un_80305D00`, reading normalized stick X from controller pad status with a deadzone.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder stubs with matched C implementations in Kirby code, including Final Cutter enter functions and Neutral-B inhale distance helpers for item and fighter victims.
Added missing includes such as `ft/ftanim.h`, `ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.h`, and `it/it_26B1.h` to support newly decompiled call sites.
Matched Kirby copied Jigglypuff Rollout collision transitions and a turnaround helper that flips rotation, velocities, facing-related move variables, and rollout vector fields.
Matched `fn_80170110` in `gm_16F1.c`, using `lbl_803D5A4C` bonus table data, a sentinel `kind == 0x29A`, flag masking, and dispatch to `pl_80039418` or `fn_801701C0`.
Matched `gm_801BEDA8` in `gm_1BA8.c`, wrapping `css->match_type` backward from 0 to `0x17` or forward modulo 24, then copying CSS data into global VS mode data.
Matched `fn_8017F09C` in `gmregclear.c`, returning a nonnegative `timer_seconds * data->x108` bonus only when several match/result flags permit it.
Renamed `FUN_801f91ec` to `grIceMt_801F91EC`, exposed it with a full typed prototype, and set up Icicle Mountain joint animation/collision callback data.
Matched `grKongo_801D8078`, scanning `HSD_GObj_Entities->items` for item kind `0xDA` within radius `grKg_804D6980->unk28` of the stage position returned by `Ground_801C4DA0`.
Matched `IsNameUnique(s32 slot)` by iterating up to `0x78` persistent name entries, skipping invalid slots, and using `CompareNameStrings`.
Matched `un_80305D00`, which reads `HSD_PadCopyStatus[i].nml_stickX` for up to four controllers and stops when absolute stick X exceeds `0.1F`.
Updated several headers from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete return and argument types.

The PR body reports additional matched functions beyond the visible excerpt, including Kirby spit/drink animations, `un_80307828`, and `fn_8017FE54`; because the slice excerpt does not show all of those changes, they should be verified in the replacement PRs.
smart_moves: Kept ground and air Kirby Final Cutter enter functions nearly identical while preserving the exact motion-state IDs, reset fields, effect spawn, and hitlag callback setup.

Reloaded `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` after calls such as `ftAnim_8006EBA4` and after `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` before writing callbacks, a pattern that often matters for MWCC register allocation and aliasing.
Used squared-distance comparisons for inhale and Kongo barrel searches, avoiding a semantic but nonmatching sqrt-style rewrite.
Preserved distinct distance API return behavior: item inhale uses an `f64 dist` from `it_802F23AC`, while fighter inhale uses an `f32 dist` from `ftCo_800BD19C`.
Tightened prototypes in headers at the same time as implementing functions, reducing reliance on `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and ambiguous pointer types.
Converted a generic/decompiler-style `FUN_801f91ec` name into the module-address convention `grIceMt_801F91EC` and made the source/header declarations agree.
Added precise includes instead of relying on undeclared externs or broad unknown headers.
Used exact constants present in the matching code paths, including motion flags `0x0C4C5092U`, effect ID `0x494`, item kind `0xDA`, and CSS wrap bounds `0x17`/`24`.
decomp_lessons: For matching PRs, header cleanup is part of the decomp: concrete prototypes can be necessary to make call sites and return-value handling match.
Control-flow shape matters. The diff uses labels, local flag variables, repeated data fetches, scoped temporaries, and explicit assignment ordering where a simpler semantic rewrite might not match.
When a static `FUN_...` helper is identified as a real subsystem symbol, update the local forward declaration, implementation name, and public header together.
Kirby special-move code often has paired ground/air variants where only motion state and ground/air transition details differ; keep the shared reset/callback/effect sequence aligned.
Collision transition functions may need to restore fighter callbacks after `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`; do not assume callbacks survive a state transition.

Names can be misleading until call sites are checked: `IsNameUnique` returns true on `CompareNameStrings(...) == 0` in the shown code, so future work should verify whether the boolean means unique, duplicate, or 'name already exists' in callers.
For object-distance helpers, preserve both numeric precision and the exact API-specific casts/types; f32 vs f64 can affect codegen.
This PR was closed unmerged and explicitly replaced by split PRs, so future decomp work should treat this as a staging record and verify final accepted code in #2322-#2325.
naming_conventions: Module/address convention is used for unknown or partially identified functions, e.g. `fn_80170110`, `gm_801BEDA8`, `grKongo_801D8078`, and `grIceMt_801F91EC`.
Temporary decompiler names like `FUN_801f91ec` should be replaced by subsystem-prefixed names once ownership is known.
Headers keep address comments such as `/* 170110 */` next to prototypes.
Kirby functions use the `ftKb_` prefix, with `Special` vs `SpecialAir` naming for ground/air variants and `MS` identifiers for motion states.
Copied-character Kirby move code uses prefixes such as `PrSpecialN` for copied Jigglypuff/Purin rollout behavior.

Ground/air constants appear as `GA_Ground` and `GA_Air`; motion-state names include `ftKb_MS_SpecialHi1`, `ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi1`, `ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNStartL`, `ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNLoop`, and `ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNEndR`.
Typed game-object aliases matter in prototypes: the diff distinguishes `Fighter_GObj*`, `Ground_GObj*`, and `HSD_GObj*`.
Kirby data-attribute fields are named descriptively, e.g. `specialn_x_offset_inhaled`, `specialn_y_offset_inhaled`, and `specialn_inhale_velocity`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma dont_inline on` was used before `ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8`, indicating an explicit anti-inlining tactic for matching.

The PR body notes MWCC register allocation trouble in Kirby drink animations: loading both `fp` and `fp->victim_gobj` into callee-saved registers can create extra `mr` instructions, and the body specifically mentions needing four callee-saved registers `r28-r31`.
The body also notes that `getFighter()` can fix `fp` allocation but not necessarily `victim_gobj`, a useful warning for future Kirby animation matches.
`fn_80170110` preserves a `goto check`/sentinel-table flow rather than rewriting as a cleaner table lookup.
`fn_8017F09C` uses local pointers `p` and `p2`, a local `flag`, and an explicit negative-product clamp, preserving source-like branching.
Kirby enter/collision functions reacquire `Fighter*` after state-changing calls before assigning callbacks, likely preserving expected register lifetimes.
The Kongo search spells out `dx`, `dy`, `dz`, squared components, and `r * r` instead of using vector helper abstractions.
The item inhale helper casts `fp->target_item_gobj` and `&pos` to the types expected by `it_802F23AC`, preserving call signature/codegen even though the casts look awkward.
review_feedback: No formal review comments or inline review comments were present in the slice.
Because the PR was closed with `merged_at: null`, the final authoritative versions of these matches should be looked up in the replacement PRs.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PRs #2322, #2323, #2324, and #2325 for the final merged or reviewed versions of the matches staged here.
Verify `IsNameUnique` call sites to determine whether its true return means unique, duplicate, or existing name.
Check the final Kirby drink animation matches for the MWCC callee-saved register issue described in the PR body.
Confirm whether `grIceMt_801F91EC` kept the `grAnime_801C7A04(..., 0)` argument in the replacement PRs.
Look up the failed functions reported in the PR body: `grPura_80212FC0`, `grOnett_801E5194`, `pl_80038628`, `fn_8021E994`, `grCastle_801CE19C`, `InitNameEntryUIState`, and `grOnett_801E40E4`.
Search for remaining `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes adjacent to the files changed here, especially game-mode and stage helper headers.
